title: linum humile subsp. crepitans
intro: Linum humile subsp. crepitans, also known as creeping flax, is a subspecies of the Linum humile plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Linum humile subsp. crepitans include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against infections and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
